results.title: Circle circus (TEFC)
results.creator_credit: Bruce Cornwell, Katharine Cornwell and International Film Bureau
results.brief_description: Several geometric attributes of the circle are suggested by constructing circles with computer animation. Without narration. Suitable for mathematics and art students.

results.title: Circles II
results.creator_credit: Doris Chase, King Screen Productions and Perspective Films
results.brief_description: Members of the Mary Stanton Dance Ensemble perform in and around huge fibreglass circles designed by artist/filmmaker Doris Chase. A superimposition technique is used on the circles and human forms, which rotate in a of different patterns and colors. The dancers move with, around and through the kinetic sculptures. Music by George Kleinsinger. Suitable for upper secondary and tertiary levels.

results.title: Cipher in the snow
results.creator_credit: Keith J. Atkinson, Wetzel O. Whitaker and Brigham Young University
results.brief_description: A child asks to be let off the school bus - and dies in the snow. Neither students nor teachers know him - his favourite teacher, who barely remembers him - goes to see his parents. In an attempt to write an obituary the teacher reads through the child’s school reports. The people around him changed him into a nobody. Designed for teachers to motivate a concern for the needs of every child.

results.title: Cinema
results.brief_description: Afghanistan, “Switzerland of Asia”, has about twelve million inhabitants and twelve cinemas. To one of these cinemas, the admission is one afghani. (Accorded the Award of Merit, Oberhausen).

results.title: Cinder in your eye
results.creator_credit: Phillip Mark-Law and Tasmanian State Film Unit
results.brief_description: An atmospheric and poetic treatment of the restoration of railway engines accompanied by the natural sounds and music of the banjo and harmonica. Locomotives and historic rolling stock once again reign in Tasmania’s superb countryside in this journey into nostalgia.

results.title: Cicada
results.creator_credit: Division of Motion Pictures Extension Service
results.brief_description: The life cycle of the cicada, or, as it is commonly known, the seven-year locust. In spring, the pupa emerges. Time-lapse photography shows us the transition from pupa to winged insect, including the shedding of the pupal shell. The winged insect lays eggs, from which young insects hatch and drop to the ground to begin their underground larval stage of development. In the spring, they will emerge in the pupal stage, and the life cycle will commence again. Narration by J.A. Hyslop.

results.title: Chuckwagon
results.creator_credit: Robert Barclay, Chetwynd Films and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: Produced in 1964, this film is a ringside view of the chuckwagon race, star attraction of the world-famous Calgary stampede. Once ponderous Percherons and Clydesdales thundered around the course. Now they are racers, and it takes a firm hand to guide such horsepower. “Chuckwagon” brings to the screen an exciting account of this extraordinary event. (Award: Cragnasco)
results.creator_credit_for_label: Robert Barclay, Chetwynd Films and National Film Board of Canada

results.title: The Chromosomes of man
results.brief_description: Methods of preparing cells for chromosome study in the laboratory. Photomicrography of cell division shows how chromosomes split and build up two daughter sets with the same number of chromosomes. The film also shows abnormal arrangements and concludes with the analysis used in studies of cancer.
